settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Proxy service, client config, authentication and data retention.
settings.update.source.label: Update source
settings.proxy.port.label: Listen port
settings.proxy.port.desc: Default 23456. Auto-increments when the port is in use.
settings.proxy.bind.label: Listen address
settings.proxy.bind.desc: Default is loopback only. With "LAN accessible" on, other devices can point Claude Code at this machine's IP.
settings.proxy.bind.warning: ⚠️ When enabled, any device on the same network can reach the proxy. The proxy itself does no auth — be careful on untrusted networks.
settings.proxy.autostart.label: Launch at startup
settings.proxy.needsRestart: Proxy config changed (protocol/port/listen address). Restart cc-router to take effect.
settings.proxy.mode.label: Listen protocol
settings.proxy.mode.desc: "HTTP only" is the simplest; "HTTPS only" requires importing the CA below into the system trust store; "both" exposes HTTP and HTTPS on separate ports so different clients can pick their own.
settings.proxy.httpsPort.label: HTTPS port
settings.proxy.httpsPort.desc: Default 23457. Auto-increments when the port is in use, and also steps around HTTP port conflicts.
settings.https.h2.label: HTTP/2 protocol
settings.https.h2.desc: When enabled, the HTTPS port advertises HTTP/2 via TLS ALPN — clients can negotiate h2, falling back to HTTP/1.1 on failure. When disabled, HTTP/1.1 is forced. Takes effect after restarting cc-router.
settings.https.cert.fingerprint.label: CA fingerprint (SHA-256)
settings.https.cert.fingerprint.desc: Use this to verify the cert in your system trust store is the same CA. A matching fingerprint means the import succeeded.
settings.https.cert.export.label: Export CA certificate
settings.https.cert.export.desc: Export cc-router's self-signed CA public key. Both .pem and .crt are written (identical content). On Windows, double-click the .crt to launch the certificate install wizard; on macOS/Linux either .pem or .crt works. Once imported into the system trust store, browsers and clients will trust the localhost certificates cc-router issues.
settings.https.cert.exportOk: Exported both .pem and .crt of the CA. Follow the steps below to import it into your system trust store.
settings.https.cert.regenerateConfirm: Regenerate the leaf certificate? This does not affect the CA already imported into your trust store; the new cert takes effect after restarting the app.
settings.https.cert.regenerateOk: Leaf certificate regenerated. Takes effect after restarting cc-router.
settings.https.cert.sans.label: Extra SANs (IP / hostname)
settings.https.cert.sans.desc: One IP or hostname per line (e.g. 192.168.1.5 or my-laptop.local). localhost / 127.0.0.1 / ::1 are always in the cert — this list appends to them. The leaf cert regenerates when this field loses focus; restart cc-router for it to take effect.
settings.https.cert.sans.regenerated: Leaf cert regenerated with the new SANs. Takes effect after restarting cc-router.
settings.https.cert.howto.title: Import into the system trust store (one-time after export — every cert cc-router issues afterwards will be trusted)
settings.https.cert.howto.macos: Double-click the .pem → Keychain Access → select "cc-router local CA" → right-click "Get Info" → expand "Trust" → set "When using this certificate" to "Always Trust" → close the window and confirm with your password.
settings.https.cert.howto.windows: Double-click the .crt → Install Certificate → choose "Local Machine" (admin required) → "Place all certificates in the following store" → browse to "Trusted Root Certification Authorities" → Finish.
settings.https.cert.howto.linux: Copy the .crt to /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/, then sudo update-ca-certificates. Firefox/Chromium use their own trust stores — import separately in browser settings.
settings.auth.token.label: Token authentication
settings.auth.token.desc: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN to this token when launching Claude Code. After regenerating, every configured client must be updated.
settings.auth.token.enabled: Enabled — requests must carry a valid token
settings.auth.cors.label: CORS
settings.auth.cors.descEnabled: Enabled — responses carry CORS headers. Set a specific value like http://localhost:5173 to allow only that origin.
settings.auth.cors.descDisabled: Disabled — browser cross-origin calls will be blocked.
settings.auth.cors.statusDisabled: Disabled — browser cross-origin calls will be blocked
settings.storage.retention.label: Request log retention
settings.storage.dbLimit.label: Database size limit
settings.language.label: UI language
settings.language.desc: Choose the cc-router UI display language. "Follow system" reads your system locale: Chinese → 中文, Japanese → 日本語, otherwise → English.
settings.debug.mode.label: Debug mode
settings.debug.mode.desc: When on, every outbound request writes three .txt files to the debug-dumps directory: the client request body, cc-router's rewritten request body, and the upstream response body. Useful for protocol adaptation issues. Files contain full conversation content — turn off and clean up after debugging.
settings.debug.dumps.label: Dump directory
settings.debug.dumps.desc: Files accumulate over time; please clean up periodically.
settings.debug.clear.dialog.title: Clear debug-dumps?
settings.debug.clear.dialog.desc: This permanently deletes all .txt files under the debug-dumps directory. Samples you've already shared with Claude for debugging are safe to remove.
settings.danger.desc: Erase all subscriptions, API keys, virtual model bindings, request logs and settings. The app restarts into the initial welcome page. This cannot be undone.
settings.danger.dialog.title: Confirm factory reset?
settings.danger.dialog.intro: The following will be permanently deleted:
settings.danger.dialog.item.logs: Request logs and model list cache
settings.danger.dialog.outro: The app will restart automatically when done.
about.title: About
about.subtitle: Project info & license
about.description: A local HTTP proxy that aggregates multiple LLM subscriptions into a single Anthropic Messages API endpoint, letting Claude Code switch between them transparently.
about.updater.debManual: · deb packages don't support in-place upgrade; please reinstall manually
about.updater.ready.title: Update ready
about.updater.ready.desc: Takes effect after restart. Restarting will briefly interrupt Claude Code's current session — do it at a convenient time.
about.disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
about.disclaimer.usage: This tool only switches between subscription plans you already own. Request bodies are passed through almost verbatim — no reverse engineering, no jailbreak, no circumvention. You are responsible for complying with each plan's terms of service. cc-router is intended for use with coding tools like Claude Code only; do not use it for anything else.
about.disclaimer.tos: Provider terms of service do not necessarily allow "routing a subscription key through a third-party proxy with multi-virtual-model dispatch" — especially for per-seat subscriptions like Coding Plans / Token Plans, where this pattern may trip risk controls. The author assumes no liability for any account being throttled, banned, or having its subscription cancelled as a result of using this tool.
about.disclaimer.warranty: This software is provided As-Is, without warranty of any kind. The author is not liable for any direct or indirect damages arising from its use, including but not limited to abnormal quota consumption, data loss, or business interruption.
onboarding.disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
onboarding.disclaimer.subtitle: Please read the following before you start using cc-router.
guide.title: Setup guide
guide.subtitle: Use cc-router as an Anthropic Messages-compatible endpoint for various clients.
guide.proxy.title: Proxy listen address
guide.proxy.intro1: cc-router runs a local HTTP proxy that aggregates subscriptions into a single Anthropic Messages endpoint. Port, listen address and auth token can be changed in
guide.proxy.intro2: (port and address changes require restarting the app).
guide.method1.title: Method 1 · Write to settings.json (recommended)
guide.method1.sub: Most reliable, no shell environment dependency
guide.method1.desc: Merge the JSON below into Claude Code's config file. If the file doesn't exist, create it.
guide.method1.note1: is filled in with the current cc-router token, ready to use. The token can be regenerated in
guide.method2.title: Method 2 · Environment variables (alternative)
guide.method2.sub: GUI-launched clients may not see shell env — prefer Method 1
guide.method2.note3: or your PowerShell profile, restart your terminal, then launch Claude Code.
guide.others.title: More integrations coming
guide.ccswitch.entry.title: Add a new provider in cc-switch
guide.ccswitch.entry.body: In cc-switch → click "Add new provider" → switch to the "Claude providers" tab → choose "Custom configuration" (don't pick one of the presets).
guide.ccswitch.method1.title: Method 1 · Fill in the form
guide.ccswitch.method1.desc: Fill in the fields below. Already populated with cc-router's current port and token — click each value to copy.
guide.ccswitch.method1.modelMapTitle: Expand "Advanced options" and fill in "Model mapping":
guide.ccswitch.method1.note: Don't add a trailing slash to the endpoint URL — cc-switch will reject it.
guide.ccswitch.method2.title: Method 2 · Paste JSON directly
guide.ccswitch.method2.desc: Paste the JSON below into cc-switch's "Config JSON" editor — sets everything in one go. Already populated with cc-router's current port and token.
guide.openclaw.path.title: Config file path
guide.openclaw.config.title: Full config example
guide.openclaw.config.desc: Write the content below into the file (create it if missing). Filled in with cc-router's current port and token, ready to use.
guide.openclaw.note.modelRef: Model refs must use the anthropic/<model-name> prefixed form. cc-router exposes model-opus / model-sonnet / model-haiku as virtual models; any other model name passes through to fallback.
guide.openclaw.note.tokenHint: The token can be regenerated on the Settings page.
guide.hermes.path.title: Config file path
guide.hermes.path.note: Or generate it interactively with the hermes model command — no manual editing required.
guide.hermes.config.title: Config example
guide.hermes.config.desc: Write the content below into the config file. Filled in with cc-router's current port, ready to use.
guide.hermes.auth.title: API Key configuration
guide.hermes.auth.desc: Use the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable, or run hermes auth add anthropic --type api-key --api-key <token>. The token is shown and regenerated on the Settings page.
guide.hermes.note.apiMode: api_mode must be anthropic_messages (the option in Hermes that matches cc-router's protocol). Anything else mismatches the protocol and 4xx's everything.
guide.opencode.path.title: Config file path
guide.opencode.path.note: Pick either project-level opencode.json or the global ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json.
guide.opencode.config.title: Config example
guide.opencode.config.desc: The @ai-sdk/anthropic npm package declares this as an Anthropic-protocol endpoint. Filled in with cc-router's current port and token, ready to use.
guide.opencode.note.baseUrlTrap: ⚠️ Don't append /v1 to baseURL. @ai-sdk/anthropic auto-appends /v1/messages internally, so doubling up gives /v1/v1/messages → 404 (this is the cause of OpenCode issue #5163).
guide.opencode.note.usage: Use /models and pick cc-router/model-opus (or sonnet / haiku).
stats.title: Statistics
stats.subtitle: Global aggregation of usage, duration, and success rate. Data is rolled up daily and kept permanently — unaffected by log retention cleanup.
stats.heatmap.title: Token usage heatmap
stats.heatmap.subtitle: Daily token consumption over the last 365 days. Darker means more usage.
stats.daily.title: Daily trend
stats.daily.subtitle: Request volume by day. Green = success, red = error, gray = timeout.
stats.daily.empty: No requests in the selected range.
stats.daily.tokenSubtitle: Token volume by day, bottom to top: cache read / cache write / input / output.
stats.byVm.title: Virtual model breakdown
stats.byVm.subtitle: Requests and success rate per virtual model slot.
stats.byVm.empty: No data in the selected range.
stats.bySub.title: Subscription ranking
stats.bySub.subtitle: Click a row to jump to the logs page filtered by that subscription.
stats.bySub.empty: No subscription data in the selected range.
requestLogs.title: Request logs
requestLogs.subtitle: Recent requests through the proxy, newest first.
requestLogs.empty.none: No request logs yet. Run a Claude Code conversation through the proxy to see records.
requestLogs.detail.title: Request detail
logs.subscriptionEvents.subtitle: History of subscription state machine transitions—useful for tracing when a subscription started failing.
logs.subscriptionEvents.empty: No subscription state changes recorded yet.
logs.systemErrors.subtitle: System-level failures and aggregate 503s that don't map to a single request.
subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.subtitle: Each subscription is one vendor's API key. Status reflects health checks live.
subscriptions.empty.message: No subscriptions yet. Click "Add subscription" to get started.
virtualModels.title: Virtual models
virtualModels.subtitle1: Three fixed virtual models map to Claude Code's model slots;
virtualModels.subtitle2: is the catch-all — any other model request goes here.
virtualModels.mode.label: Mode
virtualModels.mode.sequentialHint: By priority, fail over on error
virtualModels.addButton: Add subscription to this virtual model
virtualModels.dialog.title: Select subscriptions to add
virtualModels.dialog.empty: No available subscriptions. Add one in "Subscriptions" first.
subscriptionNew.title: Add subscription
subscriptionNew.welcomeSubtitle: Add your first subscription. We'll bind it to all 4 virtual models and jump to the setup guide.
subscriptionNew.subtitle: Connect a vendor's API key to the router and bind it to virtual model slots.
subscriptionNew.customHint: Custom path: fill in connection info and model slots in one page, no two-step wizard.
subscriptionNew.customGeminiHint: Use this for Google AI Studio or any Gemini-compatible relay. cc-router translates Anthropic Messages calls into Gemini generateContent automatically.
subscriptionNew.geminiBaseUrlHint: Gemini endpoint domain root. Use https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com for AI Studio, or your relay's actual domain.
subscriptionNew.geminiMessagesPathHint: URL path template. Must contain the {model} placeholder — cc-router substitutes it with the slot's real model name per request.
subscriptionNew.geminiAuthLocked: Gemini endpoints always use the x-goog-api-key header. Just paste your API key, no auth method to pick.
subscriptionNew.customOpenaiHint: Use this for the official OpenAI /v1/responses endpoint or any OpenAI-compatible relay (one-api, new-api, etc.). cc-router translates Anthropic Messages into OpenAI Responses calls and supports multi-turn reasoning replay.
subscriptionNew.openaiAuthLocked: OpenAI Responses endpoints always use Authorization: Bearer. Just paste your API key (usually starts with sk-...), no auth method to pick.
subscriptionNew.openaiBaseUrlHint: OpenAI Responses endpoint domain root. Use https://api.openai.com for the official API, or your relay's actual domain.
subscriptionNew.openaiMessagesPathHint: URL path, usually /v1/responses. Relays may use a different path — check their docs.
subscriptionNew.customOpenaiChatHint: Use this for OpenAI /v1/chat/completions compatible services: DeepSeek, Together, Groq, Ollama, any one-api / new-api relay, and OpenAI's legacy models (GPT-4 / 4-turbo). cc-router translates Anthropic Messages into Chat Completions calls automatically, and surfaces DeepSeek R1's reasoning_content as Claude Code thinking blocks.
subscriptionNew.openaiChatBaseUrlHint: Chat Completions endpoint domain root. Examples: OpenAI https://api.openai.com, DeepSeek https://api.deepseek.com, Together https://api.together.xyz, Groq https://api.groq.com/openai, Ollama http://localhost:11434.
subscriptionNew.openaiChatMessagesPathHi: URL path, usually /v1/chat/completions. Relays may use a different path — check their docs.
subscriptionNew.openaiChatAuthLocked: OpenAI Chat Completions endpoints always use Authorization: Bearer. Just paste your API key (usually starts with sk-...; for auth-free setups like Ollama, any non-empty string works), no auth method to pick.
subscriptionNew.errGeminiPathPlaceholder: Gemini path template must contain the {model} placeholder
subscriptionNew.providerNameHint: Name shown in the dropdown — editable later.
subscriptionNew.baseUrlHint: Domain root only — do not include the /v1/messages path.
subscriptionNew.messagesPathHint: Usually /v1/messages. Some proxies use a different path.
subscriptionNew.noteHint: Local label only — never uploaded anywhere.
subscriptionNew.slotsHint: These three slots determine which real model strings cc-router routes model-opus / model-sonnet / model-haiku to.
subscriptionNew.errFillProvider: Please enter the provider display name
subscriptionNew.errFillNote: Please enter a note for the subscription
subscriptionNew.errFillSlots: Please fill in all three virtual model slot real model names
oauth.chatgpt.title: Connect ChatGPT account
oauth.chatgpt.subtitle: Use the Codex quota of your ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription. Authorization is independent from the Codex CLI and stored only inside cc-router.
oauth.chatgpt.instructions: Browser opened. Please confirm the code shown on the page matches the one below, then authorize.
oauth.chatgpt.waitingForAuth: Waiting for you to authorize in the browser…
oauth.chatgpt.connectHint: After authorization, cc-router will run Claude Code using your ChatGPT subscription quota. Your local Codex CLI is not affected.
oauth.chatgpt.tosWarning: ⚠️ ChatGPT subscriptions are intended for OpenAI's own products and the Codex CLI; third-party usage is a gray area and OpenAI reserves the right to apply account controls. Tips: use a secondary account; avoid bursty high-frequency calls; phone-number verification when authorizing a new device is the standard flow.
oauth.kiro.title: Connect Kiro account
oauth.kiro.subtitle: Use the free Claude quota from Kiro IDE (backed by AWS CodeWhisperer).
oauth.kiro.tosWarning: ⚠️ Kiro subscriptions are intended for Kiro IDE / kiro-cli usage; third-party access is a gray area and AWS reserves the right to apply account controls. Tips: avoid bursty high-frequency calls; phone-number verification when authorizing a new device is the standard flow.
oauth.kiro.importDescription: If you have logged in to Kiro IDE or run kiro-cli login, your credentials are already cached under ~/.aws/sso/cache/ and can be loaded directly.
oauth.kiro.deviceFlowDescription: Use AWS SSO OIDC Device Flow to complete Builder ID authorization in your browser (a free account is enough). On success cc-router receives the refresh_token automatically; it never enters JS.
oauth.kiro.deviceInstructions: Browser opened. Please confirm the code shown on the page matches the one below, then authorize.
oauth.kiro.waitingForAuth: Waiting for you to authorize in the browser…
oauth.kiro.connectHint: Supports either importing Kiro IDE's cached JSON or AWS Builder ID OAuth. After import you still need to confirm 4 disguise fields.
oauth.kiro.profileArnDetected: ✓ profileArn detected, will be injected on each request.
oauth.kiro.disguiseHint: These fields are sent to AWS together with the user-agent / x-amz-user-agent header on every request, mimicking a Kiro IDE client. Defaults follow cc-router upgrades; you can edit them manually (e.g. copy the real IDE's machineId to further reduce risk-control friction).
subscriptionEdit.endpointChangeWarn: Saving will re-copy connection info from this endpoint template
subscriptionEdit.keyDialog.title: Update API key
subscriptionEdit.keyDialog.desc: The new key takes effect immediately.
subscriptionEdit.deleteDialog.refByOutro: After deletion these virtual models will lose this subscription.
subscriptionEdit.deleteDialog.noRef: Deleting a subscription also removes the stored API key.
subscriptionBalance.title: Balance
subscriptionBalance.neverFetched: Not fetched yet — click the button to load the latest balance
subscriptionBalance.accountUnavailable: Account is currently unavailable (out of credit or restricted)
modelSlot.label: Model slots
modelSlot.opus.label: Opus slot
modelSlot.sonnet.label: Sonnet slot
modelSlot.haiku.label: Haiku slot
modelSlot.historicalTitle: {model} is not in the auto list — it may be a previously manual entry. Switch to manual mode to change it.
modelSlot.modelIdPh: Enter the model ID provided by the vendor
routeFlow.title: Route map
sortableSub.empty: No subscriptions. Click the button below to add.
sortableSub.passthrough: Passes the request model through unchanged
vm.opus.label: Advanced · Plan Mode
vm.sonnet.label: Default chat
vm.haiku.label: Small tasks · Tool calls
vm.fallback.label: Fallback · pass-through unknown models
validation.baseUrl: Base URL must start with http:// or https://
receipts.title: Receipts
receipts.subtitle: Render your subscription token usage as a shareable receipt-style snapshot. Pricing is hidden by default — only usage is shown.
receipts.controls.range.title: Time range
receipts.controls.display.title: Display options
receipts.controls.display.colorModeDesc: Default is monochrome (printed-receipt look). Toggle on for cream paper with dark-brown ink.
receipts.controls.display.showProviderLo: Add brand icons (Anthropic / DeepSeek / Zhipu, etc.) before the provider name on each sub-item row. Auto-grayscaled in monochrome mode.
receipts.controls.display.showCacheDesc: Hide to keep only the input / output rows for a cleaner slip.
receipts.controls.display.showCountsDesc: Hide to show only token counts; the "156×" suffix on each item disappears.
receipts.controls.display.compactTokensD: Compress token numbers into K/M form (e.g. 12.35K, 1.23M); values below 1000 stay as-is.
receipts.controls.display.groupMode.desc: Switch the slip body's aggregation dimension; By subscription / Totals only no longer group by virtual-model slot.
receipts.controls.filter.title: Filters
receipts.controls.filter.excludeDeletedD: When checked, the receipt hides usage from subscriptions that have been deleted; totals recalculate automatically.
receipts.controls.export.title: Export receipt
